maintenance.label: Routine Maintenance — Schedule at Next Visit

plain_language_translations.caliper: the part that squeezes your brake pads against the rotor to stop the car
plain_language_translations.rotor: the metal disc your brakes clamp onto to slow the vehicle
plain_language_translations.CV axle: the shaft that sends power from the engine to your wheels — it lets the wheel move up/down while still spinning
plain_language_translations.wheel bearing: a set of steel balls that help your wheel spin smoothly — worn bearings cause a grinding or humming noise
plain_language_translations.strut: a combined shock absorber and structural support for your front suspension — controls ride and handling
plain_language_translations.tie rod: connects your steering wheel to your tires — worn tie rods cause loose or wandering steering
plain_language_translations.sway bar link: keeps your car from leaning too much in corners — worn links cause clunking over bumps
plain_language_translations.timing belt: a rubber belt that keeps your engine's valves and pistons moving in sync — if it breaks, engine damage can occur
plain_language_translations.serpentine belt: a single belt that drives your alternator, AC, and power steering — if it snaps, the car breaks down
plain_language_translations.alternator: charges your battery while the car runs — a failing alternator means your battery will eventually die while driving
plain_language_translations.O2 sensor: measures exhaust gases to help the engine use fuel efficiently — a bad sensor hurts mpg and can trigger the check engine light
plain_language_translations.EGR valve: recirculates exhaust gases to reduce emissions — a clogged EGR causes rough idle and poor performance
plain_language_translations.coolant flush: replaces the antifreeze in your cooling system — old coolant becomes acidic and can corrode your radiator and hoses
plain_language_translations.differential fluid: lubricates the gears that allow your wheels to turn at different speeds (important for turns and 4WD)

estimate_narrative_structure: Why it matters (consequence of not repairing)

formatting_rules.warranty_text: All parts and labor covered by our 12-month / 12,000-mile warranty.
